services.title: Agency Proposal Systems | Standen
services.meta: Custom agency proposal systems with pricing, approvals and version history. Fixed-scope tiers from £5k to £18k, 14 to 30 days. You own 100% of the code.
services.lead: Send proposals while the buyer still remembers the call, with scope, pricing and approvals in one owned system, not another Google Doc.
services.answer: Standen builds custom proposal systems for agencies: scoped pricing, approval chains, version history and buyer-ready PDFs in one workflow. Launch builds ship in 14 days from £5,000, Scale builds ship in 21 days from £9,500, and Enterprise builds ship in 30 days from £18,000 with full code ownership.

sections.h2: Why proposals should not live in Google Docs
sections.p: Docs duplicate, drift and hide margin. A proposal system ties scope, rate cards, optional modules and sign-off to how your agency actually sells, so founders stop rewriting the same deck every week.

sections.h2: What we build in the first sprint
sections.p: One sales workflow end to end: template logic, pricing rules, internal approval, client-facing view and export. Launch tier ships in 14 days; Scale and Enterprise extend scope and timeline. No CRM replacement: the system sits where proposals actually get made.
OhMyPod went from notes to a live owned SaaS in 21 days. Agencies using Standen builds typically cut proposal turnaround from days to under an hour when the template and pricing logic are in place.

faqs.q: How much does a proposal system cost?
faqs.a: Launch starts from £5,000, Scale from £9,500, and Enterprise from £18,000, with fixed scope and full handover.

faqs.q: Do we own the code?
faqs.a: Yes: repository, deployment and documentation are transferred on handover.

faqs.q: Can this connect to our CRM?
faqs.a: Yes. We integrate with HubSpot, Pipedrive, spreadsheets or your existing stack where it saves manual copy-paste.

services.title: Agency Reporting Dashboard | Standen
services.meta: Replace monthly reporting PDFs with live client dashboards. Fixed-scope tiers from £5k, 14 to 30 days. Full code ownership.
services.lead: Pull GA4, ad platforms and CRM pipeline into one live view, and stop rebuilding the same PDF every month.
services.answer: Standen builds agency reporting dashboards that replace manual PDFs with live, client-ready views. Data from ads, analytics and CRM in one place. Launch tier ships in 14 days; Scale in 21 and Enterprise in 30 with full ownership.

sections.p: Most agencies lose 15 to 40 hours a month pulling screenshots into decks. Clients still ask for numbers that are already stale by the time the PDF lands.
Clear KPIs per client, period comparison, commentary slots and export when procurement still wants a file. Built around how your team and clients already read performance.
FX Quant Research Platform, a full research and analytics environment, shipped in 12 days. Reporting builds reuse the same disciplined scoping and weekly delivery rhythm.

faqs.q: Can clients log in securely?
faqs.a: Yes. Role-based access, per-client views and optional white-label branding.

faqs.q: Which data sources can you connect?
faqs.a: Common stacks include GA4, Google Ads, Meta, LinkedIn, HubSpot and custom APIs.

faqs.q: How is this different from Looker Studio?
faqs.a: You own the product logic and UX, not a template that breaks every time a connector changes.

services.title: Agency Client Portal Development | Standen
services.meta: Custom client portals for agencies: status, files, approvals and messaging in one owned place. Fixed-scope tiers from £5k, 14 to 30 days.
services.lead: Move delivery out of Slack threads and email, one secure portal your clients actually open.
services.answer: Standen builds client portals for agencies: project status, file sharing, approvals and in-portal messaging in a single owned product. Delivery follows Launch (14 days), Scale (21) or Enterprise (30) with full source-code handover.

sections.h2: When Slack and email stop scaling
sections.p: Clients lose track of requests. Your team chases updates. A portal gives one auditable place for status, assets and sign-off: branded as your agency.
Project timeline, deliverable upload, approval buttons and a simple message thread. Integrations with Drive or Notion only if they remove real work.
Portals reduce repetitive client questions and make handover predictable: especially when you run multiple retainers with different stakeholders.

faqs.q: Is this a white-label client portal?
faqs.a: Yes. Your brand, your domain, your data model.

faqs.q: Can we add approvals and e-sign later?
faqs.a: Yes. We scope phases so v1 ships fast and v2 extends without rebuilding.

faqs.q: Who hosts it?
faqs.a: We deploy on modern infrastructure and hand over access: you are not locked to our hosting.

services.title: Custom CRM for Agencies | Standen
services.h1: Internal CRM and delivery queues for agencies
services.meta: Custom internal CRM for agencies: lead routing, delivery queues and team dashboards. Fixed-scope tiers from £5k, 14 to 30 days. You own the codebase.
services.lead: Route leads, track delivery and surface blockers in one workspace your team actually opens.
services.answer: Standen builds internal CRMs and ops dashboards for agencies: lead routing, delivery queues and founder views in one tool shaped to your process. Launch tier ships in 14 days; larger builds use Scale or Enterprise timelines with full ownership on handover.

sections.p: Pipelines designed for SaaS sales do not match agency delivery. Teams work around the tool in spreadsheets and five browser tabs.
Leads, projects, owners, blockers and margin signals: only the fields your operators use daily. No feature bloat.
When the same handover breaks every week, software is often the cleaner hire than another operations person copying data between tools.

faqs.q: Will this replace HubSpot?
faqs.a: Usually it complements marketing CRM: we own the delivery and ops layer HubSpot was never meant to run.

faqs.q: Can founders see pipeline and workload?
faqs.a: Yes. One dashboard for sales, delivery and what needs attention today.

faqs.q: How fast can we go live?
faqs.a: Launch tier: 14 days for a focused first version. Scale (21 days) and Enterprise (30 days) when you need more roles, integrations or compliance.

services.title: SaaS MVP Development | Standen
services.h1: SaaS MVP development for founders and agencies
services.meta: Build a SaaS MVP in 14 to 30 days. Production-ready, fixed-scope tiers from £5k to £18k. OhMyPod live in 21 days. You own 100% of the code.
services.lead: From scoped idea to live product with auth, billing-ready architecture and full handover, not a no-code prototype you cannot scale.
services.answer: Standen builds SaaS MVPs for founders and agencies in 14–21 day sprints: fixed scope, private live link from day one, full code and deployment handover. OhMyPod shipped in 21 days.

sections.h2: How we scope an MVP that ships
sections.p: One core outcome, one user type, one revenue or validation path. Everything else waits for phase two, so you launch while the market still cares.
Repository, hosting access, environment notes and a walkthrough. No black box. No licence fee to us.
OhMyPod, a podcast SaaS, live in 21 days. Scrapr.io processes 1,000+ verified leads in ~30 seconds as an owned product.

faqs.q: How much does a SaaS MVP cost?
faqs.a: Launch starts from £5,000, Scale from £9,500, and Enterprise from £18,000. Fixed quote before build starts.

faqs.q: Do you work with non-technical founders?
faqs.a: Yes. Daily demos and plain-language updates throughout the sprint.

faqs.q: Can you add subscriptions later?
faqs.a: Yes. We structure v1 so Stripe or similar can slot in without rewriting core logic.

services.title: Internal Tool Development for Agencies | Standen
services.meta: Internal tool development for agencies: workflows, integrations and bespoke ops software. Fixed-scope tiers, 14 to 30 days. Full code ownership.
services.lead: Replace spreadsheet glue and manual handoffs with software shaped to how your team already works.
services.answer: Standen builds internal tools for agencies: automation, integrations, queues and bespoke ops software in fixed-scope sprints (14, 21 or 30 days by tier). Clients own 100% of code, IP and deployment.

sections.h2: What counts as an internal tool
sections.p: Lead routers, QA checklists, resource planners, approval chains, data pipelines, anything your team rebuilds weekly in Sheets.

sections.h2: Build vs buy for agency ops
sections.p: When five SaaS products still need manual glue, a single owned tool often pays back in months. See our build vs buy guide.
Instagram lead scraper in 8 days. Crypto news aggregation in 6 days. Lead magnet generator in 9 days.

faqs.q: Is this custom software development?
faqs.a: Yes, same engagement model as our SaaS builds, scoped to internal users.

faqs.q: Can you integrate with our stack?
faqs.a: Slack, Notion, HubSpot, Google Workspace, ad APIs and custom databases.

faqs.q: What if we only know the pain, not the spec?
faqs.a: Start with the agency ops audit, we map the workflow before quoting.

comparisons.title: Agency Build vs Buy: Custom Software vs SaaS | Standen
comparisons.h1: Custom build vs off-the-shelf SaaS for agencies
comparisons.meta: Should your agency build custom software or buy SaaS? Build vs buy guide for proposals, reporting, portals and internal ops.

comparisons.title: Build Software vs Hire Ops | Standen
comparisons.h1: Build internal software vs hiring another operations person
comparisons.meta: Should your agency hire another ops person or build owned software first? Compare cost, speed and long-term margin with Standen.
comparisons.lead: When the same handover breaks every week, a scoped internal tool often beats another salary, and compounds.

sections.h2: When hiring ops is the right move
sections.p: You need human judgment on client relationships, culture and ad-hoc firefighting, and volume is still manageable in spreadsheets.

sections.h2: When software is the cleaner hire
sections.p: The same data gets copied between five tools. Reporting takes days. Proposals stall in Docs. A scoped build (from 14 days on Launch) removes recurring glue work permanently.
A £5k–£18k owned system vs £40k+ annual ops salary, plus the system does not leave when someone moves on.

faqs.q: Can we do both?
faqs.a: Yes. Software handles repeat workflow; ops focuses on clients and exceptions.

faqs.q: How fast can software ship?
faqs.a: Launch tier ships in 14 days; Scale in 21 and Enterprise in 30 when scope requires it.

comparisons.title: Custom CRM vs Spreadsheets for Agencies | Standen
comparisons.h1: Custom CRM vs spreadsheets for agency delivery
comparisons.meta: When agencies should move from spreadsheets to a custom CRM or ops dashboard, timelines, cost and ownership.
comparisons.lead: Spreadsheets work until delivery complexity makes them the risk, wrong version, no audit trail, no single view for founders.

sections.p: Multiple tabs nobody trusts. No owner on rows. Founders cannot see blockers without a meeting.

sections.h2: What a custom CRM gives you
sections.p: One queue, clear owners, status your team updates daily, and reporting that does not require export gymnastics.
A focused CRM v1 fits the Launch tier from £5,000 and typically ships in 14 days. Scale or Enterprise applies when the CRM needs more roles, integrations or compliance-oriented architecture.

faqs.q: Will my team actually use it?
faqs.a: We design around existing habits, fewer fields, faster updates than maintaining a sheet.

faqs.q: Can we migrate existing data?
faqs.a: Yes. CSV import and one-time migration scripts are common in scope.
